page: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8252C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, translating to an exceptionally high resource density of 32.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a high-speed baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks highlight strong capabilities in memory-intensive operations and specific processing tasks. The server achieves a strong Passmark CPU score of 49425.07 and a strong Passmark memory score of 2940.23. Memory bandwidth tests show strong performance, reaching 210.08 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 98.02 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 31.99 GB/sec. However, the system exhibits performance trade-offs in machine learning workloads; LLM text generation speeds are weak to poor, yielding 7.8 tokens per second on medium 7B models and 40.51 tokens per second on small 135M models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x2iezn.metal represents a high-resource-density hardware profile tailored for workloads that demand massive memory footprints alongside high single-core CPU frequencies. Because it lacks local storage, users must factor in the cost and performance of external network-attached storage. This bare-metal configuration is best suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large-scale relational databases, high-throughput memory caching, and heavy data-processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8252C processor running at 4.5 GHz. It delivers a massive 1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 32.0 GB of RAM per core, but includes no local storage or GPUs. The server features a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates strong memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds at 210.08 GB/sec and write speeds at 98.02 GB/sec, alongside a strong Passmark CPU score of 49425.07. Conversely, LLM text generation performance is weak, processing only 7.8 tokens per second on a 7B model. This high-density, bare-metal instance is designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large databases, and high-throughput data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.metal is a bare-metal server with 48 vCPUs, 1536 GB DDR4 RAM, a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x2iezn.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 48 vCPUs, 1.5 TB of DDR4 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency bare-metal server featuring massive memory density and high-speed networking for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by a 4.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8252C processor
1536.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 32.0 GB of RAM per core
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs
Strong memory bandwidth of 210.08 GB/sec read and 98.02 GB/sec write
Suited for large databases, memory-intensive enterprise apps, and caching layers
